SilveryReact for modern terminal apps

Powerful apps. Polished UIs. Proudly terminal.

Get Started
The Silvery Way
View on GitHub

Familiar React API

React 18 + 19 — hooks, refs, effects, suspense, context, portals. Flexbox layout.98.6% Ink compatible — same Box/Text/useInput you already know.Ink comparison →

Best-in-Class Performance

3–27× faster than Ink in mounted rerenders. 10–20× less terminal output.Flexily layout (or Yoga). Pure TypeScript, zero native deps. Bun and Node.js.Benchmarks →

Web-like Responsive Layout

useBoxRect() — real dimensions during render.Scroll containers — virtualization + sticky positioning.ANSI compositing — color blending with alpha.Layout guide →

Inline, Fullscreen, or Both

Fullscreen — alt screen, traditional TUI.Inline — dynamic scrollback, native Cmd+F.Virtual inline — alt screen + app-managed scrollback.Runtime layers →

Web-like Interaction

100+ protocols — full keyboard and mouse, auto-negotiated.Focus scopes — spatial nav, Tab, click-to-focus.Text selection. Find. Copy-mode. Drag-and-drop.Interaction guide →

Rich Component Library

45+ components — TextInput, SelectList, Table, TreeView, CommandPalette, ModalDialog, Tabs, Toast, and more.38 palettes — semantic tokens, auto-detected terminal colors.Browse components →

Playwright-Style Testing

CSS locators — cell-level color assertions, press() input.Termless — full ANSI fidelity, 10 swappable backends.3,000+ tests with full access to terminal internals.Testing guide →

Composable Architecture

pipe() — every layer independently swappable via providers.Layout — Flexily or Yoga.Term — real, headless, emulator.App — from stringify to rich (withFocus, withDomEvents, withCommands).Providers guide →

Are you an LLM? View /llms.txt for optimized Markdown documentation, or /llms-full.txt for full documentation bundle

Why Silvery? ​

Silvery grew out of building a complex terminal app — a multi-pane workspace with thousands of nodes. Components needed to know their size during render. Updates needed to be fast. Scroll containers, mouse events, focus scopes, and Playwright-style testing needed to just work. What started as a renderer grew into a layout engine, then 45+ components, theming, testing infrastructure, and eventually a framework.

Along the way, three principles emerged. Take the best from the web, stay true to the terminal, and raise the bar for developer ergonomics, architecture composability, and performance.

→ The Silvery Way · Silvery vs Ink · About

Build Any Terminal App ​

Try the interactive examples:

bash
npx silvery examples
bash
bunx silvery examples
bash
pnpm dlx silvery examples
bash
vp silvery examples
  • Components -- 45+ ready-made widgets: SelectList, Tabs, ProgressBar, Spinner, and more
  • Layout -- CSS flexbox for terminals: responsive sizing, gap, scroll containers
  • Forms & Input -- Multi-step wizards, SelectList, TextInput with readline
  • Tables & Data -- Table component, VirtualList, responsive columns, search/filter
  • Scrollback -- Dynamic inline mode: freeze-and-scroll, natural history (unique)
  • AI Coding Agent -- Streaming output, tool calls, command introspection for agents
  • Testing -- Headless renderer, Playwright-style locators, press() simulation

Packages ​

PackageDescription
silveryComponents, hooks, renderer — the one package you need
@silvery/ink / @silvery/chalkInk compatibility — 99% of Ink 7.0 tests, 100% of Chalk tests
@silvery/testPlaywright-style testing — locators, press(), buffer assertions
@silvery/createComposable app builder — pipe() providers (under active development)
@silvery/theme38 palettes, semantic tokens ($primary, $muted), auto-detect
@silvery/commanderBeautiful CLIs for free — help renders through Silvery itself
@silvery/headlessPure state machines — portable, embeddable, no React
@silvery/ansiTerminal primitives — styling, SGR, truecolor, detection

Quick Start ​

bash
npm install silvery react
bash
bun add silvery react
bash
pnpm add silvery react
bash
yarn add silvery react
tsx
import { useState } from "react"
import { render, Box, Text, useInput } from "silvery"

function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0)
  useInput((input) => {
    if (input === "j") setCount((c) => c + 1)
  })
  return (
    <Box borderStyle="round" padding={1}>
      <Text>Count: {count}</Text>
    </Box>
  )
}

await render(<Counter />).run()

Ecosystem ​

Standalone projects Silvery builds on — each stands on its own:

  • Flexily — pure JS flexbox layout engine (Yoga-compatible, 2.5× faster, zero WASM)
  • Termless — headless terminal testing, like Playwright for terminal apps
  • terminfo.dev — terminal feature compatibility database (161 features, 19 terminals)
  • Loggily — structured logging + tracing + metrics in one library

Coming ​

  • Pretext — rich text layout with word-wrap, hyphenation, and proportional fonts (via Flexily integration)
  • Renderers — Canvas 2D, Web DOM (experimental today, production later)
  • Frameworks — Svelte, Solid.js, Vue adapters
  • @silvery/create — structured state management with commands, keybindings, effects-as-data

Layout by Flexily · Tested with Termless · Compatibility at terminfo.dev

Built by Bjørn Stabell
